Patch Set 11:

> Patch Set 11: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
Hi, Joseph,
we just to check the original ves message to check whether it is need to do kpi computation, and no matter whether the ves message to do kpi computation or not, the original ves message  will still publish to MR，and it does not influence the already existing function of the pm-mapper. if the ves message kpi related, it is only to do simple computation and generate a kpi ves message to publish MR which is the same topic of PERFORMANCE_MEASUREMENTS。

and for this release, I hope we can do this like it. and later we can consider it as a independent feature.

Patch Set 11:

Another question is, do you have the CSIT changes made for this commit? I realise the image isn't built and available, however, I'd like to see the CSIT updates so I can build the image locally and try the new CSIT's too.

Patch Set 11:

Also have you executed the existing Bulk PM tests against an OOM deployed ONAP to verify the previous functionality remains working?

Patch Set 11:

I'd like a demo to see how you can deploy the service with the previous existing functionality ONLY in operation, and then see how you can enable the new functionality.

Patch Set 11:

> Patch Set 11:
> 
> I'd like a demo to see how you can deploy the service with the previous existing functionality ONLY in operation, and then see how you can enable the new functionality.

there is no diff between the new pm-mapper and the previous pm-mapper for deploying, installing and operations, for new pm-mapper, the change is to add a kpi_config property in pm-mapper blueprint, and kpi computation will do computation based on the configuration. Before pm-mapper publish ves data to MR, pm-mapper add a logic to check whether the ves data should do kpi computation(based on the eventName and measTypes defined in the cbs configuration), if so, will do kpi computation and generate the ves result and publish to MR(the same topic). and whether the ves data will do kpi computation or not, the original will still publish to MR

Patch Set 11:

> Patch Set 11:
> 
> Another question is, do you have the CSIT changes made for this commit? I realise the image isn't built and available, however, I'd like to see the CSIT updates so I can build the image locally and try the new CSIT's too.

 I didnt have CSIT changes until now, and maybe I can share you some samples for u to try it in your env:)

Patch Set 12: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-services-pm-mapper-master-verify-java/199/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-services-pm-mapper-master-verify-java/199

Patch Set 12:

> Patch Set 11:
> 
> > Patch Set 11: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> Hi, Joseph,
> we just to check the original ves message to check whether it is need to do kpi computation, and no matter whether the ves message to do kpi computation or not, the original ves message  will still publish to MR，and it does not influence the already existing function of the pm-mapper. if the ves message kpi related, it is only to do simple computation and generate a kpi ves message to publish MR which is the same topic of PERFORMANCE_MEASUREMENTS。
> 
> and for this release, I hope we can do this like it. and later we can consider it as a independent feature.

I think this a design fault, I don't think we should merge that much technical debt.
I wasn't saying to move it into its own application or anything, just to move the KPI compute code into its own class, that way it leaves the original publishing mechanism in tact.
I don't think its a good idea to replace a method that was originally intended just to publish to MR, with a method designed to publish to MR but first do a lot of computation on it. it really defeats the original intent for this method.

Config
KpiJsonConversion
BiFunctionExtension

KPI Computation
This entire package needs better coverage its currently sitting at 7 lines covered.
